basics.name: Kristen Such
basics.label: Aspiring Roboticist
basics.summary: A roboticist with a passion for advancing autonomy in robotics and AI, particularly in environmental and agricultural applications. Focused on long-term data association, collaborative multi-robot systems, and perception-based navigation.

work.name: University of Colorado Boulder
work.summary: Leading research in the Autonomous Robotics and Perception Group, working on projects related to environmental restoration and language-guided robot navigation.
highlights: Developed RestoreBot, a platform for navigating degraded grasslands using Mask RCNN for vegetation and microsite identification.
Designed the Language-Guided Seeding Interface for intuitive, high-level control of seeding robots using natural language.
Contributed to decentralized multi-robot SLAM and target tracking systems.

work.name: IBM
work.position: Summer Research Intern - Nanoscale Fabrication Group
work.summary: Contributed to the Electronic Nose project, which aimed to train AI models for odor fingerprinting, enhancing modular gas sensors for industrial and medical applications.

education.summary: Long-term data association for agricultural applications under the supervision of Christoffer Heckman.
Graduated with honors, Dean’s List, and UAA All-American.

publications.name: Restorebot: Towards an Autonomous Robotics Platform for Degraded Rangeland Restoration
publications.summary: Presented at ISER 2023, this paper details the development of Restorebot, a platform designed for navigating and identifying microsites in degraded rangelands.

publications.name: CogExplore: Contextual Exploration with Language-Encoded Environment Representations
publications.summary: Under review, this paper introduces a framework for language-guided exploration in unmapped environments.
